channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Apple fixes bug that cops used to extract deleted chat messages from iPhones
item.description: The iPhone and iPad bug allowed law enforcement using forensic tools to read messages that had long been deleted by the Signal app.

item.title: Ransomware negotiator recruited by BlackCat ransomware gang pleads guilty to 2023 attacks, faces 20 years in prison
item.description: The third co-conspirator pleads guilty, and now faces up to 20 years in prison.

item.title: I've picked the top 14 deals from Amazon's surprise Tech Week sale — get up to 30% off Asus, Shark, Shokz, Garmin, and more
item.description: There's a surprise Tech Week sale now underway at Amazon UK, so I've looked through the offers and picked out the 14 best deals I'd buy.

item.title: LG's first RGB TV starts at $5000 and is available to pre-order today
item.description: LG has announced the pricing and availability of its Micro RGB evo, the company's first take on a TV display trend that kicked off in earnest at CES 2026. The LG Micro RGB evo is available to pre-order today starting at $5,000, and follows the recent release of the ultra-thin LG Wallpaper.The Micro RGB evo represents the top of the line of a new class of display at LG that directly builds on the company's work with Mini LED technology. The new TV features LG's Micro RGB panel and its Alpha A11 AI processor, which runs the TV's webOS software, and perhaps more importantly, powers the "Micro RGB...

item.title: Former MrBeast exec sues over ‘years’ of alleged harassment
item.description: A former employee of Jimmy "MrBeast" Donaldson has filed a lawsuit alleging that she faced "intentional infliction of emotional distress" from harassment at the YouTuber's production company, was asked to work during maternity leave, and was wrongfully fired just a few weeks after returning from leave. According to the lawsuit, plaintiff Lorrayne Mavromatis and other […]...

item.title: 'A tabletop window into extreme physics': How graphene is breaking a fundamental law to unlock quantum computing
item.description: Researchers observed graphene violating a long-established physics law as heat and electrical conductivity diverged under extreme quantum conditions.

item.title: From the stage to the future: Where are Startup Battlefield’s alumni now?
item.description: We wanted to show you what happens after the confetti falls. We checked in with some of our recent alumni, many of whom have sat down with us on Build Mode: The Founder Survival Guide, TechCrunch's podcast for founders at every stage.

item.title: Google Cloud launches two new AI chips to compete with Nvidia
item.description: Google's newest TPUs are faster and cheaper than the previous versions. But the company is still embracing Nvidia in its cloud — for now.

item.title: OpenAI launches Privacy Filter, an open source, on-device data sanitization model that removes personal information from enterprise datasets
item.description: In a significant shift toward local-first privacy infrastructure, OpenAI has released Privacy Filter, a specialized open-source model designed to detect and redact personally identifiable information (PII) before it ever reaches a cloud-based server. Launched today on AI code sharing community Hugging Face under a permissive Apache 2.0 license, the tool addresses a growing industry bottleneck: the risk of sensitive data "leaking" into training sets or being exposed during high-throughput inference.By providing a 1.5-billion-parameter model that can run on a standard laptop or directly in a web...

item.title: 5 AI Models Tried to Scam Me. Some of Them Were Scary Good
item.description: The cyber capabilities of AI models have experts rattled. AI’s social skills may be just as dangerous.

item.title: 5 things John Ternus won’t change as Apple CEO when he takes over from Tim Cook (and 2 things he might)
item.description: Here’s what John Ternus will keep the same when he becomes Apple’s next CEO (and what he will change).

item.title: Ember’s self-heating smart mug is more than $50 off ahead of Mother’s Day
item.description: Coffee is what gets many of us through the workday, and a proper smart mug can keep it hot for hours so you can maintain your focus without constantly getting up to reheat your cup. Thankfully, if you don’t own one, the 14-ounce Ember Mug 2 is now available for $97.49 ($52 off) from Amazon, […]

item.title: Geekom's 'tiny powerhouse' A7 Max AI Mini PC for creators, home offices and power users gets a massive price cut
item.description: Geekom's "tiny powerhouse" A7 Max AI Mini PC is powered by an AMD Ryzen 9 7940HS processor and 16GB DDR5 RAM, and it's yours for $150 off.

item.title: Watch Sony’s elite ping-pong robot beat top-ranked players
item.description: Humans have been building ping-pong playing robots for decades, such as Omron's FORPHEUS that challenged amateur competitors at CES 2017. What sets Ace apart from the rest is that the robot, which was developed by Sony's AI division, is the first that can hold its own against top-ranked human players and occasionally even beat them […]

item.title: Vampire Survivors developer Poncle is opening more studios and has over 15 games in the works
item.description: Vampire Survivors developer Poncle has big plans for the future, according to an interview The Game Business conducted with the company's chief strategy officer Matteo Sapio. It's opening two new studios in Japan and Italy and has over 15 games in active development. That's a lot of action for a company primarily known for one franchise. Sapio says the company is developing three basic types of games. There are spinoffs to Vampire Survivors, like this week's deckbuilder Vampire Crawlers. Poncle is also making original IPs and says there are two games set in new universes coming down the pike. ...

item.title: I've hunted out a selection of super solar-powered gadgets that stay juiced up with the energy of the sun
item.description: So long as the sun is shining, these clever gadgets will keep on running.

item.title: Good news, EV buyers — the average price of electric cars just undercut petrol for the first time ever
item.description: The case for buying an EV over a petrol car is now even stronger as new data reveals a significant shift.

item.title: Apple MacBook Air M5, Decathlon Elops 900 E et Amazfit Active 2 Square : les réductions tech immanquables du jour (meilleurs bons plans)
item.description: Ce mercredi 22 avril 2026, les meilleurs bons plans high-tech concernent des produits aussi variés qu’un ordinateur portable Apple, un vélo électrique Decathlon et une montre connectée Amazfit.

item.title: Google turns Chrome into an AI co-worker for the workplace
item.description: Google brings Gemini-powered "auto browse" capabilities to Chrome for enterprise users, letting workers automate tasks like research, data entry, and more.

item.title: Everyone says this movie is terrible and it’s still about to make $70 million
item.description: The ‘Michael’ biopic is getting dragged everywhere, but the box office is exploding. Michael is aiming for a record-breaking opening weekend. It’s also a critical flop.
